title: Pronoun person
description: Ask yourself: "Who is this sentence talking about?"  Then choose the pronoun that makes the most sense.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which pronoun makes the most sense in this sentence?**

>When you go on a trip,  \_\_\_\_\_\_ should pack plenty of layers.

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
Pronoun person distinguishes among "me/us" (first person), "you" (second person), and "he, she, one, it, they, etc." (third person).

The pronoun we select should agree in person with two things: 1) the noun the pronoun describes, and 2) other pronouns used in the sentence.
This sentence uses the second person pronoun "you" elsewhere in the sentence. So, we should use the pronoun **"you"** again to match.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which pronoun makes the most sense in this sentence?**

>I couldn’t sleep because  \_\_\_\_\_\_ kept convincing myself there was something outside.

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
Pronoun person distinguishes among "me/us" (first person), "you" (second person), and "he, she, one, it, they, etc." (third person).

The pronoun we select should agree in person with two things: 1) the noun the pronoun describes, and 2) other pronouns used in the sentence.
This sentence uses the first person pronoun "I" elsewhere in the sentence. So, we should use the pronoun **"I"** to match.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which pronoun makes the most sense in this sentence?**

>As a student,  \_\_\_\_\_\_ can prepare yourself for tests by studying.

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
Pronoun person distinguishes among "me/us" (first person), "you" (second person), and "he, she, one, it, they, etc." (third person).

The pronoun we select should agree in person with two things: 1) the noun the pronoun describes, and 2) other pronouns used in the sentence.
This sentence uses the second person pronoun "yourself" elsewhere in the sentence. So, we should use the pronoun **"you"** to match.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which pronoun makes the most sense in this sentence?**

>This is Jennifer; I met  \_\_\_\_\_\_ at the swim meet.

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
Pronoun person distinguishes among "me/us" (first person), "you" (second person), and "he, she, one, it, they, etc." (third person).

The pronoun we select should agree in person with two things: 1) the noun the pronoun describes, and 2) other pronouns used in the sentence.
In this sentence, the pronoun refers back to "Jennifer", so we should use the third person pronoun **"her".**

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which pronoun makes the most sense in this sentence?**

>Cleo and Harry are a great team because  \_\_\_\_\_\_ always stick together.

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
Pronoun person distinguishes among "me/us" (first person), "you" (second person), and "he, she, one, it, they, etc." (third person).

The pronoun we select should agree in person with two things: 1) the noun the pronoun describes, and 2) other pronouns used in the sentence.
In this sentence, the pronoun refers back to "Cleo and Harry", so we should use the third person plural pronoun **"they".**

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which pronoun makes the most sense in this sentence?**

>Paul was so tired he could barely keep  \_\_\_\_\_\_ eyes open.

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
Pronoun person distinguishes among "me/us" (first person), "you" (second person), and "he, she, one, it, they, etc." (third person).

The pronoun we select should agree in person with two things: 1) the noun the pronoun describes, and 2) other pronouns used in the sentence.
In this sentence, the pronoun refers back to "Paul", so we should use the third person possessive adjective **"his".**

The text uses the second person pronoun "your" in the next sentence. So, we should use the pronoun **"you"** to match.
